justification: All pass criteria met: zero-downtime dual-write strategy clearly designed; phases defined including backfill, dual-write, shadow-read, PG-primary, and decommission; rollback plan at each phase via feature flag rollback; MongoDB data shape inconsistency risk explicitly identified in Risks table and Pitfalls section; trade-offs table includes dual-write latency and operational overhead.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough phase design with 5 distinct phases (off, dual-write, shadow-read, pg-primary, pg-only) exceeding the expected 7-step breakdown
Backfill risk thoroughly addressed: cursor-based pagination, upsert semantics, checkpoint resumability, and critical ordering constraint (dual-write before backfill)
MongoDB document schema variance explicitly called out as High severity risk with mitigation (field usage audit, DocumentTransformer handling missing/extra/renamed fields)
Rollback plan is feature-flag-based requiring no deployment, with MongoDB kept as rollback safety net for 2 weeks after PG-primary
Trade-off analysis includes CDC vs dual-write decision with clear rationale for choosing dual-write
Concrete backfill math: 8M records at 1K batch size, ~67 min estimate, with headroom analysis against 200 writes/min production load
Pitfalls section adds practical guardrails (don't use skip/limit, don't make PG writes synchronous, don't skip shadow-read phase)
PostgreSQL schema design is concrete with actual SQL, indexes, constraints, and reporting views

Shadow-read phase is a valuable addition but slightly reorders the expected phases (backfill before dual-write in rubric vs dual-write before backfill here) — though MJ's ordering is arguably more correct since dual-write must precede backfill to avoid data gaps

agent_output_excerpt:
